007e7d6cb64a7dfcdcad9fc6f4f0ba69fdc88203: LLMs offer significant breakthroughs in AI but face challenges with cloud reliance, including long response times, high bandwidth costs, and privacy concerns.
Collaborative edge computing (CEC) is proposed as a solution, integrating geo-distributed edge devices and cloud servers to address LLM resource intensiveness and memory requirements.
EdgeShard is introduced as a framework for efficient collaborative LLM inference on heterogeneous edge devices and cloud servers.
EdgeShard employs dynamic programming algorithms to optimize LLM inference by jointly selecting devices and partitioning the model to minimize latency or maximize throughput.
Experimental results demonstrate that EdgeShard reduces latency by up to 50% and achieves 2x throughput compared to on-device and vertical cloud-edge collaborative inference methods.
929305892d4ddae575a0fc23227a8139f7681632: State-of-the-art LLMs (GPT-4, Claude v1.3) remain highly susceptible to jailbreak attacks despite extensive safety training and red-teaming efforts.
The study identifies two core failure modes of safety training: competing objectives and mismatched generalization.
New attacks designed based on these failure modes outperform existing ad hoc jailbreaks, succeeding on 100% of curated unsafe requests and over 96% of a larger dataset.
The findings emphasize the necessity of safety-capability parity and argue against scaling alone as a solution for these safety failure modes.
